generated_from: scripts/scrape-venice-categories.json — Phase 1 baseline; refine after initial-scrape data is in

venice.golden_lion.notes: Golden Lion (Leone d'Oro) — single winner per ceremony in modern era. Tolerance 1 allows for the rare hypothetical tie.

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.notes: Maps to Wikipedia's 'Grand Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)' — the second-tier non-director prize. Awarded in essentially every ceremony in the 2000-2025 scope. Tolerance 1 allows for ties.

venice.silver_lion_best_director.notes: Maps to Wikipedia's 'Silver Lion' (target [[Silver Lion]]). Modern era (2013+): person-first 'Silver Lion for Best Director' format. Pre-2013: typically film+director second-tier prize without Best Director qualifier. Tolerance 1 allows for ties.

venice.special_jury_prize.notes: Distinct from Grand Jury Prize — Venice has both, with Special Jury Prize awarded intermittently (not awarded in cer 2000 and possibly other early years). Tolerance 1; era_overrides may be added if initial-scrape data shows extended gap years.

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.notes: Volpi Cup for Best Actor. Cannes-style ties have occurred occasionally; tolerance 1 covers.

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.notes: Volpi Cup for Best Actress. Cannes-style ties have occurred occasionally; tolerance 1 covers.

venice.best_screenplay.notes: Premio Osella for Best Screenplay. Display name varies historically ('Golden Osella for Outstanding Technical Contribution (screenplay)' in older years). Tolerance 1 covers ties / co-author teams.

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.notes: Spotty history at Venice — has been retired and reinstated; expect 0-row years for some ceremonies. Tolerance 1 allows for ties; era_overrides will likely be added once gap years are identified from initial scrape.

venice.lion_of_the_future.notes: Luigi De Laurentis Award for Debut Feature. Section-heading-driven extraction (Pass 2). Single winner expected per ceremony.
